title: practical english usage - Part 210
content:
We   put an auxiliary verb (and non-auxiliary have and be) directly before the 270 inversion:      a uxiliar    y  verb    befor    e  subjec    t I'll leave it to you to decide.

We owe it to society to make our country a better place.

Note the structures owe it to somebody to... and leave it to somebody to ...

4 owe     and   leave if you would.. .) not I would appreciate it if you would keep me informed. (     I would appreciate It is used as a preparatory object for an if-clause after would appreciate.

3 I would       appreciate           it  if  ...

grammar «271     inversion: full verb before subject Along the road came a strange procession.                 -> Directly in front of them stood a great castle.

On  the grass sat an enormous frog.

Under ɑ  tree was lying one of the biggest men I had ever seen.

most common in literary and descriptive writing.

especially when a new indefinite subject is being introduced. The  structure is clause, intransitive verbs are often put before their subjects. This happens When  an adverbial expression of place or direction comes at the beginning of a 1  after    adverbial       expressions         of  place:   Along      the    road      came      ...

271 inversion:      f ull  verb    before     subje   ct Not long after that she got married.

Not far from here you can see foxes, (     Netfrtffrem-here-e&nryou-.. .) Inversion is not used after not far... and not long...

Not a single word did he say.

Not only did we lose our money, but we were nearly killed.

Only after her death was I able to appreciate her.

Only then did I understand what she meant.

Never... was so much  owed by so many to so few. (Churchill) Little did he realise the danger he faced.

Seldom have I seen such a remarkable creature.

Hardly had I arrived when trouble started. (BrE) little and never, and after only + time expression. This is formal or literary.

Inversion is also used after restrictive words like hardly (in BrE), seldom, rarely, Not until he received her letter did he folly understand her feelings.

The  same structure is possible after a complete clause beginning not until...

Not until much  later did she learn who her real father was.

At no time was the President aware of what was happening.

Under no circumstances can we accept late payment.

are mostly rather formal.

for emphasis, it is usually followed by auxiliary verb + subject. These structures If a negative adverb or adverbial expression is put at the beginning of a clause 7 after    negative       and   restrictive       expressions:        At  no   time     was     he   . ..

Had we not spent all our money already,... (     Hadn't we spent...) Negatives are not contracted in this case.

Hadi realised what you intended... (= If ɪ  had realised ...) Were she my daughter... (= If she were my  daughter ...) the subject instead of using if  244.5).

In formal and literary conditional clauses, an auxiliary verb can be put before 6 conditional        clauses:     Were      she    my    daughter         ...

So ridiculous did she look that everybody burst out laughing.

City dwellers have a higher death rate than do country people.

She was very religious, as were most of her friends.

Inversion sometimes happens after as, than and so in a literary style.

5 after    as,   than      and   so Information StructureSection 25 grammar •  272  fronting: This question we have already discussed.

Strange people they are!  Very good lesson we had yesterday.

This happens mostly in speech.

Fronting words in short sentences can also give them extra emphasis.

2  Very     good      lesson       we    had.

How she got the gun  through customs we never found out.

Wftat I'm going to do next I just don't know.

Question-word clauses (►   266) are often fronted.

putting strong emphasis on me.) (A) fat lot of good that does me. (= ‘ That doesn’ t do me much good’ , but People like that I just can't stand.  These books I'm just going to give away.

Fronting is particularly common in speech.

Any video in our catalogue we can supply, if available, (notice in music shop) (from a business letter) All the other information which you need I am sending today.
